what_it_does: Root agent onboarding document for Counterpart Studio that instructs agents to read CLAUDE.md first, adds local context, lists the single-source token registries, maps the folder structure, and describes the edit-and-open workflow.
observations: CLAUDE.md is declared the binding spec and wins over any AGENTS.md contradiction.
This file and subfolder AGENTS.md are meant to add local detail, not restate rules.
It restates one rule: never hardcode a value already owned by a registry or token.

Single sources are global window objects defined in src/tokens.js, including MS_TOKENS, MS_STATUS, MS_SPACE, MS_RADIUS, MS_ELEVATION, MS_TYPE, MS_MOTION, MS_REGISTRY, MS_TOAST, MS_DATA, plus the ViMark and Eyebrow components.
Registries are required to fail loudly on unknown keys and must not have silent fallbacks.
Each folder has its own AGENTS.md for localized agent context.
The workflow is edit src/*.jsx or styles/*.css, then open Counterpart Studio.html; there is no build step.
standouts: The central rule against hardcoding any value owned by a registry or token.
The explicit loud-failure policy for registry lookups.
The hierarchy where CLAUDE.md is binding and AGENTS.md files only add local detail.
conventions: Hierarchical agent onboarding with a root doc and per-folder AGENTS.md files.
Single-source-of-truth design tokens exposed as global window objects.
Loud failure instead of silent fallback for registry lookups.
Plain Markdown with bold labels and blockquotes for emphasis.
concerns: The file says AGENTS.md files never restate the rules, then immediately restates one rule, creating a local tension.
No build step is mentioned but script load order is delegated to another AGENTS.md, so this file alone does not explain how to verify correctness mechanically.
notes: Root agent orientation doc that anchors the codebase in CLAUDE.md and the token-registry no-hardcoding discipline.
questions: What is the exact content and enforcement mechanism of CLAUDE.md?
How do the subfolder AGENTS.md files relate to this root file and what local details do they add?
What does opening Counterpart Studio.html verify, and are there automated checks?
What is the full API and key set of each token registry?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches: it is a root orientation doc that adds local detail. It makes one explicit exception by restating the core no-hardcoding rule.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for design tokens and domain values.
Agent onboarding and portable folder-level context.
intention_signals: To create a self-documenting, folder-traveling agent context system that keeps design-token discipline and prevents hardcoded values across a static HTML/JS/React codebase.
novelty: Folder-local AGENTS.md files that carry agent mentality with the working directory, plus an explicit registries-fail-loud policy rather than silent fallbacks.
contribution: Provides the root agent orientation and the token-registry/no-hardcoding rule that a fused system would keep as the entry point for understanding codebase conventions.
summary_for_embedding: Root AGENTS.md onboarding file for Counterpart Studio that directs agents to CLAUDE.md, enforces token-registry usage, maps folders, and describes the no-build workflow.
intention_for_embedding: To orient AI agents to the project's binding spec, token discipline, and folder-level context conventions.
